## US Supreme Court Justice Retirement/Resignation — Research Report (May 2026)

### ⚠️ Data Limitation Notice
All external research tools (WebSearch, WebFetch, ToolSearch) are unavailable in this session. The following is based on training data available up to early 2025, supplemented by contextual reasoning about the political environment. **I cannot confirm any events that occurred in 2026.**

### Background: Key Justices and Retirement Speculation

**Justice Clarence Thomas**
- Born June 23, 1948 (age 77 as of May 2026)
- Appointed by President George H.W. Bush in 1991
- As of early 2025, there was significant speculation that Thomas might retire during a Republican presidency (Trump's second term began January 2025) to allow a like-minded replacement
- Thomas faced ethics controversies in 2023-2024 regarding undisclosed gifts and travel from wealthy benefactors

**Justice Samuel Alito**
- Born April 1, 1950 (age 76 as of May 2026)
- Appointed by President George W. Bush in 2006
- Similar speculation existed about Alito potentially retiring during the Trump administration
- Alito faced controversies regarding flags flown at his residences

**Justice Sonia Sotomayor**
- Born June 25, 1954 (age 71 as of May 2026)
- Appointed by President Obama in 2009
- As of early 2025, there was progressive pressure on Sotomayor to retire while Democrats still had some political leverage (though this was complicated by Republican Senate control after 2024)
- Health concerns (Type 1 diabetes) have been publicly discussed

### Political Context (as of early 2025)
- President Trump took office January 2025 with a Republican Senate majority
- This created a political environment favorable to conservative justices retiring with confidence of like-minded replacements
- Liberal justices like Sotomayor faced a difficult calculus — retiring under a Republican president/Senate would mean replacement by a conservative

### What I Cannot Confirm
- Whether any justice has actually announced retirement or resignation in 2026
- Any health developments for any justice
- Any formal statements from the justices about their plans
- Current court composition as of May 2026
